title: These Chip Stocks Could Gain 40% This Year And Outperform The S&P 500
text: JP Morgan reports that chip stocks typically rise 40% one year after entering a new uptrend. The post These Chip Stocks Could Gain 40% This Year And Outperform The S&P 500 appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.

title: 4 Factor Dividend Growth Portfolio - January 2023 Update
text: The 4 factor dividend growth portfolio was launched on November 1st, 2022, and is thus far off to a great start. The portfolio lost by 11 bps to the S&P 500 Total Return during the month of January.

text: US stocks closed mixed on Friday, with the Dow Jones gaining around 170 points during the session.

title: Why Monolithic Power Systems Powered Up an 8% Gain This Week
text: The combination of an earnings beat and a dividend raise proved very tempting for investors.

title: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (MPWR) Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:MPWR ) Q4 2022 Earnings Conference Call February 8, 2023 5:00 PM ET Company Participants Genevieve Cunningham – Marketing Communications Manager Michael Hsing – Chief Executive Officer and Founder Bernie Blegen – Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Conference Call Participants Quinn Bolton – Needham Tore Svanberg – Stifel Matt Ramsay – Cowen Gary Mobley – Wells Fargo Ross Seymore – Deutsche Bank Rick Schafer – Oppenheimer William Stein – Truist Chris Caso – Credit Suisse Genevieve Cunningham Welcome, everyone, to the MPS Fourth Quarter 2022 Earnings Webinar. My name is Genevieve Cunningham, and I will be the moderator for this webinar.

title: Monolithic Power (MPWR) Surpasses Q4 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Monolithic (MPWR) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 1.28% and 0.02%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Can Healthy Revenue Growth Aid Monolithic (MPWR) Q4 Earnings?
text: Despite top-line improvement driven by rising demand, Monolithic's (MPWR) earnings are likely to be affected by macroeconomic uncertainty and supply chain headwinds in the semiconductor market.
